Clean energy investors relieved by Trump tax rule changes Late on Friday, the Treasury Department narrowed the definition for what it means for a solar or wind project to be considered under construction, a requirement to qualify for federal tax credits worth 30% of a projects cost.  The changes include requiring developers of big solar arrays and wind farms to complete physical work rather than simply show that they have invested capital.

Fridays Treasury guidance helped spell out when projects will count as “under construction,” which determines which wind and solar farms can claim tax credits.  The Trump administration is phasing out tax credits early, and after a tough year for the sector, some feared the rules could be changed retroactively.
